Btrfs Checkpoint Volume Requirement

The BlockDAG stack uses IPFS as the recovery transport for signed raw datadir checkpoints and append-only chain-order segments. IPFS content addressing makes objects immutable once published, but it does not make a hot filesystem copy safe by itself. The stack therefore needs a local checkpoint substrate that can preserve cheap point-in-time generations before sealing and publishing recovery artifacts.

Default Install Behavior

On Linux, ops/release-install.sh installs btrfs-progs when needed, creates a loop-backed btrfs image by default, adds an /etc/fstab entry, mounts it at ./data-restore/btrfs-checkpoints, and creates the sidecar directories under that mount. The default image is 128 GiB and the installer refuses to create it unless the image filesystem can retain the configured free-space reserve afterward.

PurposeDefault Path
Mutable low-priority raw sidecar./data-restore/btrfs-checkpoints/rawdatadir-sidecar/mainnet
Signed sealed raw artifacts./data-restore/btrfs-checkpoints/rawdatadir-artifacts
Chunked raw content for IPFS publication./data-restore/btrfs-checkpoints/rawdatadir-sidecar-content
Open restore generations./data-restore/btrfs-checkpoints/rawdatadir-sidecar-open/mainnet

Why This Is Required

The live node database may remain on ext4, F2FS, XFS, or another suitable chain-data filesystem. The recovery sidecar is different: it must support cheap checkpoint generations so the stack can keep recent restore points, sign and verify raw checkpoint artifacts, and publish to IPFS without repeatedly full-copying the chain or competing aggressively with mining I/O.

When BDAG_IPFS_STATE_CHECKPOINT_REQUIRED=1, preflight treats ext4-only sidecar/artifact/open-restore paths as a deployment blocker. A native btrfs, ZFS, or LVM-backed checkpoint volume is acceptable when the operator configures the paths explicitly; otherwise the installer provisions the loop-backed btrfs volume.

Trust Boundary

IPFS CIDs are immutable content addresses. They are not enough on their own to prove chain correctness. A raw checkpoint is usable only after its manifest root, file/chunk hashes, Ed25519 signature, mainnet metadata, trusted signer roster, and chain-anchor evidence pass verification. Consumers must still validate restored data against normal chain consensus before mining on it.

Operational Checks

findmnt ./data-restore/btrfs-checkpoints
./scripts/mining-appliance-preflight.py --root . --env-file .env --warn-only --enforce-blockers
systemctl --user status bdag-rawdatadir-sidecar.timer bdag-ipfs-content-sidecar.timer
